question.content: For the C,N two-dimensional catalyst support, Au single-atom doping is known to occupy one carbon-defect site and forms an Au-N3 coordination with three neighboring nitrogen atoms. Besides this known site, count how many other inequivalent C/N defect-site types can host a single Au atom (ignore lattice distortion). Answer format: a single integer; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single integer; write only the number itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Use the lattice and local coordination to classify inequivalent atomic positions around the defect environment rather than counting repeated symmetry-equivalent atoms.
step_1.key_point: Count only distinct C/N defect-site classes.

step_3.content: Treat the Au-N3 carbon-defect configuration as the already-given one. Then inspect the remaining nonequivalent nearby C/N positions that define other possible single-defect occupations.
step_3.key_point: Exclude the known Au-N3 carbon-defect site from the final count.

step_5.content: After removing that known class, the remaining inequivalent defect-site classes are three in number.

step_7.content: Therefore the count of additional defect-site types is three.
step_7.key_point: Final single expression is the integer count.
